questions.stem: The old bridge ______ last year because it was too dangerous for cars.
questions.explanation: 选A。本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。主语The old bridge是动作repair的承受者，且时间状语last year表示过去，因此用was/were + 过去分词的结构。B是主动语态；C是一般现在时被动，时态不符；D是第三人称单数主动形式。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: Do you know the girl ______ won the first prize in the English speech contest?
questions.explanation: 选A。本题考查定语从句关系代词。先行词the girl指人，且在从句中作主语，因此用who。which指物；whom指人但作宾语；whose表示所属关系。口诀：先行词指人作主语用who，作宾语用whom/who/that，指物用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —______ you ever ______ to the Shenzhen Museum? —Yes, I went there last month with my classmates.
questions.explanation: 选B。本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。ever是现在完成时的标志词，表示"曾经"，强调过去的经历对现在的影响，结构为have/has + 过去分词。答句中的last month是明确的过去时间，用一般过去时，但问句用现在完成时询问经历。A是一般过去时的疑问句式，不能与ever搭配表示经历；C是一般现在时；D是现在进行时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new school library ______ by the end of next month. We can't wait to use it.
questions.explanation: 选B。本题考查一般将来时的被动语态。by the end of next month是将来时间，且library与complete是被动关系，因此用will be + 过去分词。A是主动语态；C是一般现在时被动，表示经常性动作或状态；D是现在完成时被动，表示已完成的动作，与next month矛盾。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you ______ the letter 'r' from the word 'friend', it becomes 'fiend'.
questions.explanation: 选A。remove意为"移除、去掉"，前缀re-表示"向后、离开"，move表示"移动"，合起来就是移开。return返回；reply回复；recycle回收利用。根据句意"去掉字母r"，选remove。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: As students, we should ______ those who are in trouble and offer them help.
questions.explanation: 选A。look after意为"照顾、照料"；look for寻找；look up查阅（词典）或向上看；look like看起来像。根据语境"帮助有困难的人"，应该是"照顾"他们，因此选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It's important to ______ a good habit of reading books for 30 minutes every day.
questions.explanation: 选B。develop a habit是固定搭配，意为"养成习惯"。grow通常指种植或自然生长；increase指数量增加；raise指举起、筹集或养育。develop除了发展，还有"逐渐形成"的含义，与habit搭配最恰当。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to take part in it.

The project (8)______ to reduce waste and save energy in our school. First, we put special bins in every classroom for different kinds of waste. Many students were not used to it at first, but soon they (9)______ how important it was to separate rubbish.

We also checked the lights and computers in empty classrooms. If we found any lights (10)______ with no one inside, we turned them off. In the first week, we saved about 20% of electricity. Our headteacher was very (11)______ with our work.

The most interesting part was making old things useful again. We collected empty bottles and turned them into flower pots. It was amazing to see how "rubbish" could become beautiful decorations. Through this project, I've learned that environmental protection starts with small (12)______ around us.
questions.explanation: 选A。aim to do sth. 意为"旨在做某事、目的是做某事"，主语可以是project/program等。ask要求，通常搭配ask sb. to do；hope希望，主语通常是人；let让，搭配let sb. do。这里指项目的目的，用aimed最恰当。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to take part in it.

The project (8)______ to reduce waste and save energy in our school. First, we put special bins in every classroom for different kinds of waste. Many students were not used to it at first, but soon they (9)______ how important it was to separate rubbish.

We also checked the lights and computers in empty classrooms. If we found any lights (10)______ with no one inside, we turned them off. In the first week, we saved about 20% of electricity. Our headteacher was very (11)______ with our work.

The most interesting part was making old things useful again. We collected empty bottles and turned them into flower pots. It was amazing to see how "rubbish" could become beautiful decorations. Through this project, I've learned that environmental protection starts with small (12)______ around us.
questions.explanation: 选A。realize意为"意识到"，符合语境"起初不习惯，但很快意识到垃圾分类的重要性"。remember记得；forget忘记；decide决定。根据上下文逻辑，从"不习惯"到"理解重要性"，用realized表示认知上的转变。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to take part in it.

The project (8)______ to reduce waste and save energy in our school. First, we put special bins in every classroom for different kinds of waste. Many students were not used to it at first, but soon they (9)______ how important it was to separate rubbish.

We also checked the lights and computers in empty classrooms. If we found any lights (10)______ with no one inside, we turned them off. In the first week, we saved about 20% of electricity. Our headteacher was very (11)______ with our work.

The most interesting part was making old things useful again. We collected empty bottles and turned them into flower pots. It was amazing to see how "rubbish" could become beautiful decorations. Through this project, I've learned that environmental protection starts with small (12)______ around us.
questions.explanation: 选A。lights on表示"灯亮着"，是固定搭配。根据后文"turned them off（关掉它们）"可知，前面应该是发现灯还亮着。off表示关闭；up向上；down向下。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to take part in it.

The project (8)______ to reduce waste and save energy in our school. First, we put special bins in every classroom for different kinds of waste. Many students were not used to it at first, but soon they (9)______ how important it was to separate rubbish.

We also checked the lights and computers in empty classrooms. If we found any lights (10)______ with no one inside, we turned them off. In the first week, we saved about 20% of electricity. Our headteacher was very (11)______ with our work.

The most interesting part was making old things useful again. We collected empty bottles and turned them into flower pots. It was amazing to see how "rubbish" could become beautiful decorations. Through this project, I've learned that environmental protection starts with small (12)______ around us.
questions.explanation: 选B。be pleased with是固定搭配，意为"对……感到满意"。前文提到节省了20%的电力，所以校长应该是"满意的"。angry生气的；sad难过的；worried担心的，均与语境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. As a member of the environmental club, I was excited to take part in it.

The project (8)______ to reduce waste and save energy in our school. First, we put special bins in every classroom for different kinds of waste. Many students were not used to it at first, but soon they (9)______ how important it was to separate rubbish.

We also checked the lights and computers in empty classrooms. If we found any lights (10)______ with no one inside, we turned them off. In the first week, we saved about 20% of electricity. Our headteacher was very (11)______ with our work.

The most interesting part was making old things useful again. We collected empty bottles and turned them into flower pots. It was amazing to see how "rubbish" could become beautiful decorations. Through this project, I've learned that environmental protection starts with small (12)______ around us.
questions.explanation: 选A。small steps意为"小步骤、小行动"，引申为"点滴行动"。句意：环保始于我们周围的小行动。grades年级、分数；marks分数、标记；results结果。start with small steps是常见表达，强调从身边小事做起。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time looking at screens than ever before. Recent studies show that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 6-8 hours on screens daily on average. This has led to a serious problem: more and more young people are suffering from eye strain (疲劳) and myopia (近视).

To solve this problem, Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, created the "20-20-20" rule. According to this rule, for every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple practice helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

"Many students think eye exercises are enough, but they're not," says Dr. Li. "The key is to give your eyes regular breaks. When you stare at a screen, you blink less often, which makes your eyes dry."

However, following the 20-20-20 rule is not always easy. Some students find it hard to remember to take breaks when they are concentrating on games or videos. To help with this, there are now special apps that remind users to rest their eyes every 20 minutes.

Dr. Li also suggests spending more time outdoors. "Natural light is good for eye health," he explains. "Studies show that children who play outside for two hours a day have a lower risk of developing myopia."

By following these simple tips, teenagers can protect their eyes while still enjoying their digital devices.
questions.stem: According to the passage, how many hours do Shenzhen teenagers spend on screens daily on average?
questions.explanation: 选C。细节理解题。根据文章第一段第二句"Recent studies show that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 6-8 hours on screens daily on average"可知，深圳青少年平均每天花6-8小时在屏幕上。A、B、D选项均与原文数据不符。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time looking at screens than ever before. Recent studies show that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 6-8 hours on screens daily on average. This has led to a serious problem: more and more young people are suffering from eye strain (疲劳) and myopia (近视).

To solve this problem, Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, created the "20-20-20" rule. According to this rule, for every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple practice helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

"Many students think eye exercises are enough, but they're not," says Dr. Li. "The key is to give your eyes regular breaks. When you stare at a screen, you blink less often, which makes your eyes dry."

However, following the 20-20-20 rule is not always easy. Some students find it hard to remember to take breaks when they are concentrating on games or videos. To help with this, there are now special apps that remind users to rest their eyes every 20 minutes.

Dr. Li also suggests spending more time outdoors. "Natural light is good for eye health," he explains. "Studies show that children who play outside for two hours a day have a lower risk of developing myopia."

By following these simple tips, teenagers can protect their eyes while still enjoying their digital devices.
questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li Ming's words in Paragraph 3?
options: A. Eye exercises are completely useless for protecting eyes.
B. Students should avoid using digital devices completely.
C. Blinking more often can help keep eyes moist.
D. Only doctors can help solve eye problems.
questions.explanation: 选C。推理判断题。根据第三段李医生的话"When you stare at a screen, you blink less often, which makes your eyes dry"（盯着屏幕时眨眼次数减少，导致眼睛干涩），可以反推：多眨眼有助于保持眼睛湿润。A项错误，文中说眼保健操不够（not enough），而非完全无用；B项过于绝对，文章最后一句说可以同时保护眼睛和享受数码设备；D项文中未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time looking at screens than ever before. Recent studies show that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 6-8 hours on screens daily on average. This has led to a serious problem: more and more young people are suffering from eye strain (疲劳) and myopia (近视).

To solve this problem, Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, created the "20-20-20" rule. According to this rule, for every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple practice helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

"Many students think eye exercises are enough, but they're not," says Dr. Li. "The key is to give your eyes regular breaks. When you stare at a screen, you blink less often, which makes your eyes dry."

However, following the 20-20-20 rule is not always easy. Some students find it hard to remember to take breaks when they are concentrating on games or videos. To help with this, there are now special apps that remind users to rest their eyes every 20 minutes.

Dr. Li also suggests spending more time outdoors. "Natural light is good for eye health," he explains. "Studies show that children who play outside for two hours a day have a lower risk of developing myopia."

By following these simple tips, teenagers can protect their eyes while still enjoying their digital devices.
questions.stem: The underlined word "strain" in Paragraph 1 probably means "______" in Chinese.
questions.explanation: 选B。词义猜测题。根据上下文"spend about 6-8 hours on screens"（长时间看屏幕）以及并列词"myopia（近视）"可知，strain应该是一种眼部问题或不适。后文也提到"reduce tiredness"（减少疲劳），与strain呼应。因此strain意为"疲劳、劳损"。eye strain即"眼疲劳"。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, teenagers spend more time looking at screens than ever before. Recent studies show that Shenzhen teenagers spend about 6-8 hours on screens daily on average. This has led to a serious problem: more and more young people are suffering from eye strain (疲劳) and myopia (近视).

To solve this problem, Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, created the "20-20-20" rule. According to this rule, for every 20 minutes of looking at a screen, you should look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple practice helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

"Many students think eye exercises are enough, but they're not," says Dr. Li. "The key is to give your eyes regular breaks. When you stare at a screen, you blink less often, which makes your eyes dry."

However, following the 20-20-20 rule is not always easy. Some students find it hard to remember to take breaks when they are concentrating on games or videos. To help with this, there are now special apps that remind users to rest their eyes every 20 minutes.

Dr. Li also suggests spending more time outdoors. "Natural light is good for eye health," he explains. "Studies show that children who play outside for two hours a day have a lower risk of developing myopia."

By following these simple tips, teenagers can protect their eyes while still enjoying their digital devices.
questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: A. The History of Eye Care
B. How to Make an App for Eye Health
C. The 20-20-20 Rule for Eye Protection
D. Why Teenagers Love Digital Devices
questions.explanation: 选C。主旨大意题。文章第一段提出青少年近视问题，第二段引出核心内容"20-20-20"护眼法则，第三、四段解释该法则的原理和实施难点，第五段补充建议（户外活动），最后总结。全文围绕"20-20-20法则"展开，因此C项最能概括主旨。A项范围过大；B项只是细节；D项与主题无关。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Health is very important to everyone, especially for us students. (17)______ Here are some useful tips for you to keep healthy.

First, eat a balanced diet. (18)______ Try to eat more vegetables and fruits instead of junk food like hamburgers or chips.

Second, exercise regularly. You don't need to do heavy sports. (19)______ Walking, swimming, or playing basketball for 30 minutes every day is enough.

(20)______ Try to sleep at least 8 hours every night. Don't stay up late playing computer games or watching videos.

If you follow these suggestions, you will feel better and study more efficiently.
options: A. However, many students don't know how to keep healthy.
B. In addition, getting enough sleep is necessary.
C. For example, you can walk to school instead of taking a bus.
D. This means eating different kinds of food to get various nutrients.
E. On the other hand, you should limit your screen time.
questions.explanation: 选A。考查段落衔接。前一句说健康很重要，后一句"Here are some useful tips"（以下是一些建议），中间需要过渡句引出建议。A项"然而，许多学生不知道如何保持健康"承上启下，符合逻辑。B项讲睡眠，应放在第四个空；C项是举例，应放在解释运动的空；D项解释均衡饮食，应放在第二个空。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Health is very important to everyone, especially for us students. (17)______ Here are some useful tips for you to keep healthy.

First, eat a balanced diet. (18)______ Try to eat more vegetables and fruits instead of junk food like hamburgers or chips.

Second, exercise regularly. You don't need to do heavy sports. (19)______ Walking, swimming, or playing basketball for 30 minutes every day is enough.

(20)______ Try to sleep at least 8 hours every night. Don't stay up late playing computer games or watching videos.

If you follow these suggestions, you will feel better and study more efficiently.
options: A. However, many students don't know how to keep healthy.
B. In addition, getting enough sleep is necessary.
C. For example, you can walk to school instead of taking a bus.
D. This means eating different kinds of food to get various nutrients.
E. On the other hand, you should limit your screen time.
questions.explanation: 选D。考查逻辑关系。前一句提到"balanced diet（均衡饮食）"，此处需要解释什么是均衡饮食。D项"这意味着吃不同种类的食物以获取各种营养"正好解释了balanced diet的含义。this指代前文的balanced diet。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Health is very important to everyone, especially for us students. (17)______ Here are some useful tips for you to keep healthy.

First, eat a balanced diet. (18)______ Try to eat more vegetables and fruits instead of junk food like hamburgers or chips.

Second, exercise regularly. You don't need to do heavy sports. (19)______ Walking, swimming, or playing basketball for 30 minutes every day is enough.

(20)______ Try to sleep at least 8 hours every night. Don't stay up late playing computer games or watching videos.

If you follow these suggestions, you will feel better and study more efficiently.
options: A. However, many students don't know how to keep healthy.
B. In addition, getting enough sleep is necessary.
C. For example, you can walk to school instead of taking a bus.
D. This means eating different kinds of food to get various nutrients.
E. On the other hand, you should limit your screen time.
questions.explanation: 选C。考查举例说明。前一句说"你不需要做剧烈运动"，后一句列举了walking, swimming等轻度运动，因此此处需要举例说明什么是轻度运动。C项"例如，你可以步行上学而不是坐公交车"符合语境，是对"轻度运动"的具体举例。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Health is very important to everyone, especially for us students. (17)______ Here are some useful tips for you to keep healthy.

First, eat a balanced diet. (18)______ Try to eat more vegetables and fruits instead of junk food like hamburgers or chips.

Second, exercise regularly. You don't need to do heavy sports. (19)______ Walking, swimming, or playing basketball for 30 minutes every day is enough.

(20)______ Try to sleep at least 8 hours every night. Don't stay up late playing computer games or watching videos.

If you follow these suggestions, you will feel better and study more efficiently.
options: A. However, many students don't know how to keep healthy.
B. In addition, getting enough sleep is necessary.
C. For example, you can walk to school instead of taking a bus.
D. This means eating different kinds of food to get various nutrients.
E. On the other hand, you should limit your screen time.
questions.explanation: 选B。考查段落主题句。后一句提到"sleep at least 8 hours"（睡至少8小时），因此本段主题是充足睡眠的重要性。B项"此外，获得充足睡眠是必要的"作为本段首句，引出下文关于睡眠的具体建议。in addition表示递进，引出第三条建议。
questions.label: 短文填空
